LAW AND MOTION 302, PETITIONER JESSICA RODRIGUEZ'S VERIFIED PETITION FOR WRIT OF MANDAMUS IS OFF CALENDAR. THE COURT DID NOT RECEIVE ANY COURTESY COPIES IN COMPLIANCE WITH SAN FRANCISCO LOCAL RULE 2.6B OR A PROOF OF SERVICE. JUDGE: ERNEST H. GOLDSMITH; CLERK: GINA GONZALES; REPORTER: SHERRY SAWYER, CSR# 5976.

THE COURT ORDERED THE FOLLOWING JUDGMENT ENTERED: IT IS ORDERED, ADJUDGED, AND DECREED THAT PETITIONER JESSICA RODRIGUEZ SHALL TAKE NOTHING, AND THAT JUDGMENT SHALL BE ENTERED IN FAVOR OF RESPONDENT CALIFORNIA UNEMPLOYMENT INSURANCE APPEALS BOARD. (SEE SCANNED JUDGMENT)

LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFF JESSICA RODRIGUEZ'S VERIFIED PETITION FOR WRIT OF MANDAMUS IS DENIED. THE ADMINISTRATIVE RECORD SUPPORTS THE DECISION BY THE CALIFORNIA UNEMPLOYMENT INSURANCE APPEALS BOARD THAT PETITIONER WAS DISCHARGED FOR MISCONDUCT AND IS THEREFORE NOT ELIGIBLE FOR UNEMPLOYMENT BENEFITS PURSUANT TO CAL. UNEMP. INS. CODE, SEC. 1256. THE EVIDENCE ESTABLISHES THAT PETITIONER WAS REPEATEDLY TARDY TO WORK IN VIOLATION OF HER EMPLOYER'S ATTENDANCE POLICY, EVEN AFTER SHE RECEIVED COUNSELING, WARNINGS, AND SUSPENSIONS DUE TO THE EXCESSIVE VIOLATIONS. (ADMINISTRATIVE RECORD, EX. F, TRANSCRIPT OF HEARING, EXS. 2-7 TO 2-14.) ORDER SIGNED. JUDGMENT SIGNED. JUDGE: ERNEST H. GOLDSMITH; CLERK: GINA GONZALES; REPORTER: SHERYL L. SAWYER, CSR# 5976.(302/EHG)

calendar.matters:
Ntc Of Hrg On Petition For Writ Of Mandamus
Off Calendar Oct-06-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
PLAINTIFF JESSICA RODRIGUEZ's Hearing On Petition For Writ Of Mandamus is Off calendar. Duplicative entry. (302)

Notice Of Hearing On Verified Petition For Writ Of Mandamus
Off Calendar Oct-06-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Robert C. Bowman Jr., Esq. of the Law Offices of Bowman & Associates, APC, T:916.224.0321, for petitioner and moving party;
Hadara Stanton, Esq., Deputy Attorney General, T:415.703.5561, for respondent and opposing party.

RULING: A R G U E D. At the conclusion of argument, the Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: PLAINTIFF JESSICA RODRIGUEZ's Verified Petition For Writ Of Mandamus is denied. The Administrative Record supports the Decision by the California Unemployment Insurance Appeals Board that Petitioner was discharged for misconduct and is therefore not eligible for unemployment benefits pursuant to Cal. Unemp. Ins. Code, sec. 1256. The evidence establishes that Petitioner was repeatedly tardy to work in violation of her employer's attendance policy, even after she received counseling, warnings, and suspensions due to the excessive violations. (Administrative Record, Ex. F, Transcript of Hearing, Exs. 2-7 to 2-14.) ORDER SIGNED. JUDGMENT SIGNED. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Reporter: Sheryl L. Sawyer, CSR# 5976.

Ntc Of Hrg On Petition For Writ Of Mandamus
Off Calendar Apr-21-2014 Continued To Oct-06-14 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance. The Court adopted its tentative ruling which was not contested. PLAINTIFF JESSICA RODRIGUEZ'S Hearing On Petition For Writ Of Mandamus is continued to October 6, 2014 at the request of the parties. Counsel are reminded to provide courtesy copies in advance of the hearing in compliance with Local Rule 2.6B, and to file timely proofs of service in accordance with the Rule of Court 3.1300(c). Judge: Marla J. Miller; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Reporter: Sherry Sawyer CSR# 5976.
